title: Fitting a pull-out larder drawer unit
subtitle: Install a full-extension wire basket or drawer carcase inside an existing kitchen cabinet
excerpt: How to fit a pull-out larder or pan drawer unit inside an existing kitchen base cabinet or tall larder cabinet. Covers removing the existing shelves, fitting the drawer runners, squaring the unit, and adjusting for a smooth action.
sourceNotes: Häfele drawer runner and pull-out unit installation guides (publicly available). Blum Tandem runner fitting instructions (reference format). Collins Complete DIY Manual, Albert Jackson and David Day (1989).

glossaryTerms.definition: A drawer or pull-out runner that allows the drawer or basket to extend its full depth out of the cabinet, giving complete access to the back of the unit. Standard runners extend to 75% of the cabinet depth; full-extension runners extend to 100% (soft-close versions add a damper that slows the drawer as it closes). Pull-out larder units almost always require full-extension runners.

content.text: A pull-out larder unit installs inside an existing base or tall cabinet and replaces fixed shelves with a full-depth

wire basket or drawer that extends to the full cabinet depth. One pull gives access to everything at the back. Fixed shelves cannot do this. Units from Häfele, Blum, or Ninka come with runners included. The fitting is straightforward if the cabinet dimensions match the unit.

A pull-out larder unit with runners to suit the cabinet depth (measure the internal cabinet depth from front face to back panel, and the internal width between side panels). The fixings provided with the unit (usually 20 mm pan-head screws). A cordless drill with the appropriate bit. A spirit level. A tape measure. A combination square. A pencil.

Remove all existing shelves and shelf supports. Measure the internal cabinet width (side panel to side panel) and depth (back panel to front face), and the height between the base and the underside of the next fixed shelf or top panel. The pull-out unit must have 10 to 15 mm clearance on each side and must clear the door hinges when fully extended. Compare these measurements with the unit specification before ordering or before fitting a unit already purchased.

The runners fix to the cabinet side panels. Mark the vertical position of the runners using the spirit level and a tape measure, positioning the bottom runner at the height specified in the unit instructions (usually 50 to 100 mm above the cabinet base). Mark the runner position on both side panels at the same height. Use the spirit level to ensure both marks are at identical heights; if the runners are not level, the drawer will tilt and run stiffly or not at all.
Step 3, fix the runners to the cabinet

Hold each runner against the side panel, aligning its front edge flush with the front face of the cabinet. Drill pilot holes and screw the runner to the side panel with the provided fixings. Repeat on the other side, checking the runner positions are level with each other across the cabinet. For soft-close runner systems, fit the soft-close mechanism at the rear of each runner before inserting the drawer.
Step 4, insert and adjust the pull-out unit

Slide the pull-out unit into the runners. On clip-in systems, press the drawer carcase or basket onto the runners until it clicks; pull the unit out again and check the clips are engaged. Test the action: it should slide smoothly with no tilt or binding. Make side-to-side adjustments using the lateral adjustment eccentrics on the runner (turn with a screwdriver) to centre the unit between the side panels. For stacked pull-out units (two or more baskets in one tall cabinet), fit the lower unit and its runner first, check the action, then fit the upper runner at the correct height above.

attrs.symptom: Drawer tilts or runs stiffly on the runners
attrs.cause: Runners are at slightly different heights on the two side panels.
attrs.fix: Adjust the runner height on the low side — most runners have a vertical adjustment slot. Loosen the fixing screws slightly, lift the runner to the correct height, and retighten.

attrs.symptom: Unit does not extend fully — stops short of the cabinet door
attrs.cause: Cabinet depth was not measured correctly; unit is too long for the cabinet depth.
attrs.fix: Check the unit specification against the measured internal depth. If the unit is correct, check whether a door hinge is obstructing the path.
